title: A Busy Morning
learningGoal: The learner will decode narrative text utilizing heavy clusters of literal phrasal verbs, distinguishing their context-dependent meanings related to physical actions and electronic states.
instructions: Read the story about Amit getting ready for work. Look closely at the Phrasal Verbs (two words glued together). Notice how he 'turns off' his alarm and 'puts on' his clothes. Answer the 8 questions below.

contentItems.text: Notice how the story splits some of the verbs! 'Turn the TV on' is just as correct as 'Turn on the TV'.

readingText: The sun is shining. Amit's alarm rings at 7:00 AM. He wakes up, but he is still tired. He turns off the alarm and goes back to sleep. At 7:30 AM, his mother walks into the room. 'Amit, get up! You will be late,' she says. He quickly gets up out of bed. He goes to the bathroom and turns the shower on. The cold water wakes him up completely. After his shower, he goes back to his bedroom. He puts on his blue shirt and his black pants. He goes to the kitchen and turns on the coffee machine. While the coffee is making, he puts his shoes on. He sits down at the table and drinks his coffee. Suddenly, he looks at the clock. It is 8:15 AM! He stands up quickly. He takes off his home slippers, picks up his bag, and runs out the door. He turns the lights off as he leaves.
textInTargetLanguage: The sun is shining. Amit's alarm rings at 7:00 AM. He wakes up, but he is still tired. He turns off the alarm and goes back to sleep. At 7:30 AM, his mother walks into the room. 'Amit, get up! You will be late,' she says. He quickly gets up out of bed. He goes to the bathroom and turns the shower on. The cold water wakes him up completely. After his shower, he goes back to his bedroom. He puts on his blue shirt and his black pants. He goes to the kitchen and turns on the coffee machine. While the coffee is making, he puts his shoes on. He sits down at the table and drinks his coffee. Suddenly, he looks at the clock. It is 8:15 AM! He stands up quickly. He takes off his home slippers, picks up his bag, and runs out the door. He turns the lights off as he leaves.
baseLanguageSupportText: सूरज चमक रहा है। सुबह 7:00 बजे अमित का अलार्म बजता है। वह जागता है, लेकिन वह अभी भी थका हुआ है। वह अलार्म बंद कर देता है और वापस सो जाता है। सुबह 7:30 बजे उसकी माँ कमरे में आती है। 'अमित, उठो! तुम्हें देर हो जाएगी,' वह कहती है। वह जल्दी से बिस्तर से उठता है। वह बाथरूम जाता है और शावर चालू करता है। ठंडा पानी उसे पूरी तरह से जगा देता है। नहाने के बाद वह वापस अपने बेडरूम में जाता है। वह अपनी नीली शर्ट और अपनी काली पैंट पहनता है। वह रसोई में जाता है और कॉफी मशीन चालू करता है। जबकि कॉफी बन रही होती है, वह अपने जूते पहनता है। वह मेज पर बैठ जाता है और अपनी कॉफी पीता है। अचानक, वह घड़ी की ओर देखता है। सुबह के 8:15 बज चुके हैं! वह जल्दी से खड़ा हो जाता है। वह घर की चप्पल उतारता है, अपना बैग उठाता है, और दरवाज़े से बाहर भागता है। जाते समय वह लाइट बंद कर देता है।

glossary.baseTranslation: उठना (बिस्तर/कुर्सी से शारीरिक रूप से उठना)

exampleSentence.targetText: He quickly gets up out of bed.
exampleSentence.transliteration: ही क्विकली गेट्स अप आउट ऑफ बेड।
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: वह जल्दी से बिस्तर से उठता है।
वह अलार्म बंद कर देता है।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: He puts on his blue shirt.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: ही पुट्स ऑन हिज़ ब्लू शर्ट।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: वह अपनी नीली शर्ट पहनता है।
वह जल्दी से खड़ा हो जाता है।

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What does Amit do to the alarm at 7:00 AM?
Look closely at the story. What is the difference between 'Waking up' and 'Getting up'?
options: They mean exactly the same thing.
Waking up is opening your eyes. Getting up is physically moving out of bed.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Fill in the blank from the story: He turns the shower ____.
What phrasal verb means 'कपड़े पहनना' in this story?
The story says 'He puts his shoes on'. Is this exactly the same meaning as 'He puts on his shoes'?
What does he do with his slippers?
What action does he perform with his bag?
As he leaves his house, does he say 'I will close the lights' or 'I will turn the lights off'?

correctAnswers.q2: Waking up is opening your eyes. Getting up is physically moving out of bed.

expectedSummaryPoints: Learner correctly differentiates semantic nuances between 'wake up' (neurological arousal) and 'get up' (physical elevation).
Learner comprehends splittable phrasal structures ('turns the shower on').

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You perfectly decoded the phrasal verbs! You easily understood how putting one small word next to a verb completely changes the action.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Remember to read the second word carefully. Turn ON means start a machine. Turn OFF means stop it. Put ON means dress in clothes. Take OFF means remove clothes.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Watch out for Question 2. 'Wake up' means your eyes open in bed. But 'Get up' means you physically stand up and leave the bed. This is an important native detail!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: The sun is shining. Amit's alarm rings at 7:00 AM... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Pay attention to the little words! 'On', 'Off', 'Up', and 'Down' completely change the meaning of the action.
commonMistakes: Thinking 'He puts his shoes on' is grammatically incorrect because the object splits the verb (very common in L1 speakers resisting splittable phrasals).
